machinery_genes.role: secreted hexameric inner-tube protein (hallmark substrate / tube)
machinery_genes.quote: The export of hemolysin coregulated protein (Hcp), a small 17.4-kDa protein capable of forming nanotubes in vitro, is the hallmark of T6SSs because Hcp is consistently found in the supernatant of bacteria harboring a functional T6SS.
machinery_genes.note: Per the T6SS brief Hcp is counted as machinery (secreted inner tube). In V. cholerae two hcp paralogues lie outside the main locus (hcp-1 = VC1415, hcp-2 = VCA0017 in the literature); listed once as the Hcp tube component.

machinery_genes.role: trimeric tube-tip spike (VgrG); also carries a C-terminal actin-crosslinking effector domain
machinery_genes.quote: In Vibrio cholerae, T6SS have been found to secrete three related proteins extracellularly, VgrG-1, VgrG-2, and VgrG-3.
machinery_genes.note: VgrG = valine-glycine repeat spike protein; counted as machinery (spike) per the T6SS brief even though its C-terminal ACD is an effector domain. Encoded outside the main locus (VC1416 in the literature).

machinery_genes.quote: In Vibrio cholerae, T6SS have been found to secrete three related proteins extracellularly, VgrG-1, VgrG-2, and VgrG-3.
machinery_genes.note: Shortest of the three VgrGs; Zheng et al 2011 show it is the VgrG individually essential for Hcp secretion and killing. Encoded outside the main locus (VCA0018 in the literature).

machinery_genes.quote: another two, VCA0121 and valine-glycine repeat protein G 3 (VgrG-3), are not essential for Hcp expression, secretion or bacterial virulence
machinery_genes.note: VgrG-3 = VCA0123, the only VgrG inside the main T6SS locus. Counted as spike machinery; its delivered C-terminal toxin domain (a cell-wall-degrading effector) is NOT counted. VCA0123 is also one of this instance's curated effectors, reflecting the spike/effector duality.

machinery_genes.quote: VipA binds to VipB and they together form tubules in E. coli that Leiman first noted have a striking resemblance to structures corresponding to the contracted tail sheath of bacteriophage T4.
machinery_genes.note: VipA = VCA0107 (Zheng et al 2011, Table 1 / Fig 1). Sheath component essential for Hcp secretion.

machinery_genes.quote: VipA binds to VipB and they together form tubules in E. coli that Leiman first noted have a striking resemblance to structures corresponding to the contracted tail sheath of bacteriophage T4.
machinery_genes.note: VipB = VCA0108 (Zheng et al 2011). Sheath partner of VipA; the VipA/VipB tubule is remodelled by ClpV.

machinery_genes.role: AAA+ ATPase that recycles the contracted sheath
machinery_genes.quote: an AAA+ family ATPase called ClpV
machinery_genes.note: ClpV = VCA0116 (Zheng et al 2011, Table 1). Same paper: 'ClpV of V. cholerae has previously been shown to be required for remodeling of the tubular complex formed by the products of VCA107 (VipA) and VCA108 (VipB).' Counted per the brief (ClpV/TssH is essential machinery).

machinery_genes.quote: Another mutant, SP95, carries an insertion in the VasK gene, which encodes a homolog of icmF.
machinery_genes.note: VasK = VCA0120 (Pukatzki et al 2006, Table 1). IcmF/TssM-family membrane component; MacIntyre et al 2010: 'V. cholerae cells lacking vasK are unable to export either Hcp or VgrG proteins.' Essential for Hcp secretion (Zheng et al 2011).

machinery_genes.quote: Two genes in the cluster, vasK (VCA0120) and vasF (VCA0115), show a high degree of similarity to icmF and icmH.
machinery_genes.note: VasF = VCA0115 (Pukatzki et al 2006). icmH/DotU homolog, assigned to TssL (DotU-like) membrane-complex role. Zheng et al 2011: 'VipA, VipB, VasF, ClpV and VasK are T6SS structural components essential for Hcp secretion.'

machinery_genes.role: core structural component essential for T6SS function (specific subcomplex unresolved from quote)
machinery_genes.quote: Two Dictyostelium-attenuated mutants, SP17 and SP65, carried independent TnAraOut insertions in gene vasA
machinery_genes.note: VasA = VCA0110 (Pukatzki et al 2006, Table 1). Named vas (virulence-associated secretion) structural gene of the main locus; loss attenuates T6SS-dependent virulence. Specific Tss family not assigned from the naming quote, so left as generic T6SS-core (low-confidence family call, gene identity itself is well supported).

notes: This is the single chromosome-2 'Large' T6SS locus of V. cholerae, spanning roughly VCA0105-VCA0124 on NC_002506.1, the system that delivers this instance's curated effectors (VCA0117 region effectors VC_A0117/VC_A0123). The founding T6SS work (Pukatzki et al 2006, PNAS) was done in non-O1/non-O139 strain V52 (O37 serogroup), which has a constitutively active T6SS; N16961 (sequenced reference) carries the same locus but is silent under lab conditions. Pukatzki 2006 explicitly ties the components to this reference strain: 'V52 secreted four proteins that could not be detected in supernatants of N16961, namely Hcp, VgrG-1, VgrG-2, and VgrG-3' (DOI 10.1073/pnas.0510322103) -- i.e. the apparatus genes are present in N16961, transfer is direct (identical VCA locus tags). Status PARTIAL: I list the 10 apparatus genes that have a verbatim, locus-resolved naming quote (Hcp; VgrG-1/-2/-3; sheath VipA/VipB; ATPase ClpV; membrane VasK/VasF; core VasA). Zheng et al 2011 show that the additional locus genes VCA0109 and VCA0111-VCA0114 and VCA0119 are structural components essential for Hcp secretion, but the published literature does not give them individual gene NAMES (they are referenced only by VCA locus tag), so per the names-only / no-invention rule they are deliberately omitted rather than padded with fabricated vasB/vasC/etc. designations. Deliberately EXCLUDED: VasH (VCA0117) and VCA0122, which are transcriptional regulators (sigma-54 activator and a Vibrio-specific positive regulator), not apparatus structural parts; VCA0118 (putative effector/adhesin); VCA0121 (non-essential, unknown); and delivered toxin effectors / immunity proteins. VasF family assigned to TssL (DotU/IcmH-like) and VasK to TssM (IcmF) from the homology stated in Pukatzki 2006; VasA family left generic (low-confidence family call). All quotes are verbatim from the cited PMC/PLoS full texts or article abstracts; all four PMIDs and DOIs verified via get_article_metadata.
